title: TypeError: column.equivalentTo is not a function
author: christophedebatz

comments.author: kpsuperplane
comments.body: @christophedebatz how did you solve this? I'm getting the same issue

comments.author: christophedebatz
comments.body: Hi @kpsuperplane, I removed the getExportHeaderName function and I simpliefed all this code just to keep the base that work. In fact, I think it was an error in my own code in the loop (for ()) that filled each row... I was missing some data and the column key (or value, I don't remember) was null... That's why I got that error. I hope it will help you. Kiss!

comments.author: kpsuperplane
comments.body: @christophedebatz it appears you have to dump the entire columns array into the columns property at once... I was pushing to it inplace before with a for loop... reassigned it into a variable, dumped it in all at once after, and it worked

comments.author: joewinger
comments.body:
>  it appears you have to dump the entire columns array into the columns property at once

Just wanted to pop in a few years late and say that this fixed it for me too. I was trying to use `worksheet.columns.push(x)` in a for loop and having an issue. Thanks!

comments.author: LVMuser
comments.body: Hi @joewinger How did you get around this? #377

comments.author: alberttomasiak
RishabhParashar
comments.body: @alberttomasiak thanks but instead of const I used let.

comments.author: blaues0cke
comments.body: Can confirm that the problem is to call `sheet.columns.push()`. The fix/workaround is to build the column array earlier and apply it once like in the example of @alberttomasiak.

comments.author: ghost
comments.body:
> @LVMuser You can't use `worksheet.columns.push(x)` .
> 
> In order to make this work you can only define the columns once. So, to get it to work define a variable with your columns, add the extra ones afterwards and THEN define the columns on your worksheet


Thank you [alberttomasiak](https://github.com/alberttomasiak). This is the solution for me.
